CP00-811 is a clean descriptor-only failure-recovery bridge pack (risk B, 40 units). Every layer reconciles with the prompt baseline: binding, requirements, section rows, descriptor/packets, validators, tests, persisted contract, live plan (same source_ledger_sha), README, and index re-exports. Deliverable distribution (22/4/3/4/2/4/1=40) and micro-title split (20/20) match exactly, and it's a clean non-overlapping continuation from CP810 into CP00-812/RP26.P07.M03.S21.

No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries are enforced at the attestation, row, descriptor, validator, test, and contract layers; Claude stays read-only/non-final; Hermes is descriptor-only evidence, not product runtime. The manifest does not over-claim (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, review_pending).

The single finding is a non-blocking P3: downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence/construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result) and validation-command runs are not yet present — the expected mid-review state — and I verified everything by source inspection only since I'm read-only with no Bash. This mirrors the CP809/CP810 precedent.
